cross_refs: ISS-1325 (sibling instance in WIN-012 — recommended close)
summary: WIN-014 detail_evidence contains the same fabricated "E-PRED-C" prediction label that was just removed from WIN-012. No such label exists on the dome live site (verified by curmudgeon c5 H1 WebFetch on 2026-04-21). Moreover the cited value "0.0 µGal" conflicts with PRED-TIER-3, which predicts 10–13 µGal in Level-3 SG residuals for the Aug 2026 eclipse — so the current paragraph both cites a phantom ID and misses the stronger structural argument that the dome now positively predicts a signal in the instrument class WIN-014 originally treated as null-confirming.
detail: Current text in WIN-014 detail_evidence reads: "The dome now registers SG null as a prospective prediction (E-PRED-C for the August 12, 2026 eclipse: 'SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal'). A defender could argue this shows advance specification. But this is non-discriminating: standard physics also predicts null SG results during eclipses..." This has two problems: (1) no prediction on the dome site is labeled E-PRED-C (same phantom-ID defect as WIN-012.c5 H1); (2) the dome's actual Aug-2026 SG prediction is PRED-TIER-3 ("SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals" — data/predictions.json confirms), not a 0.0 µGal null. Leaving the phantom label in place risks a motivated reader searching the dome site, finding nothing, and (correctly) concluding we are citing labels that do not exist — exactly the integrity failure the c5 review flagged. Recommended fix: replace the opening sentence of the paragraph with verified PRED-TIER-3 language and pivot the argument to the structural point that the dome cannot simultaneously claim SG nulls confirm the model (WIN-014, 2009) and that SGs will show 10–13 µGal at C1/C4 in Aug 2026 (PRED-TIER-3). Full replacement text in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-206.json → companion_finding.recommended_replacement_text.
recommended_action: decider self-apply or assign-analyst for a minimal win_detail_patch using the replacement text provided in EXP-206.json
